title: America's Founding Symbols
description: A beginner-friendly coloring activity featuring important symbols from America's founding period, helping students recognize and understand key American historical icons and their significance.
instructions: Color each founding symbol using your favorite colors. As you color, discuss what each symbol means with your teacher or parent.

content.image: A collection of American founding symbols arranged on a page, including the Liberty Bell, American flag with 13 stars in a circle, bald eagle, Constitution document with 'We the People' visible at the top, Independence Hall, and the Statue of Liberty.

elements.name: Liberty Bell
elements.description: Color the famous cracked bell that was rung to announce the signing of the Declaration of Independence

elements.name: First American Flag
elements.description: Color the original flag with 13 stars arranged in a circle, representing the first 13 states

elements.name: Bald Eagle
elements.description: Color America's national bird, a symbol of freedom and strength

elements.name: Constitution
elements.description: Color the important document that begins with 'We the People'

elements.name: Independence Hall
elements.description: Color the building where the Declaration of Independence and Constitution were debated and signed

elements.name: Statue of Liberty
elements.description: Color this famous statue that represents freedom and welcomes people to America

learningFacts: The Liberty Bell rang to celebrate the reading of the Declaration of Independence in 1776
The first American flag had 13 stars for the original 13 colonies
The bald eagle became America's national symbol in 1782
The Constitution begins with the words 'We the People'
Independence Hall is in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Freedom and liberty were important ideas when America was founded
